Object.Name: USB Management
Object.Description1: This LwM2M Object provides monitoring and control of USB ports on embedded devices and routers. It supports multiple instances for managing different USB ports, providing information about connected devices, power management, and port status. Useful for OpenWRT routers with USB ports for storage, modems, or other peripherals.

Item.Name: Port Name
Item.Description: Descriptive name of the USB port (e.g., "USB1", "USB2", "Front_USB", "Type-C").

Item.Name: Port Type
Item.Description: USB port type: 0=USB 2.0 Type-A, 1=USB Type-C. This indicates the physical connector type and USB version.

Item.Name: Port Enabled
Item.Description: Enable or disable the USB port. When disabled, the port is powered down and devices are not recognized.

Item.Name: Device Connected
Item.Description: USB device connection status: true=Device connected, false=No device connected.

Item.Name: Device Name
Item.Description: Name of the connected USB device as reported by the device (e.g., "SanDisk Ultra", "Huawei LTE Modem"). Empty if no device connected.

Item.Name: Device Vendor ID
Item.Description: USB Vendor ID in hexadecimal format (e.g., "0x12d1"). This identifies the device manufacturer.

Item.Name: Device Product ID
Item.Description: USB Product ID in hexadecimal format (e.g., "0x1506"). Combined with Vendor ID, this uniquely identifies the device model.

Item.Name: Device Class
Item.Description: USB device class (e.g., "Mass Storage", "Communication Device", "Human Interface", "Hub"). This indicates the device type and function.

Item.Name: Power Enabled
Item.Description: Control USB port power supply: true=Power enabled, false=Power disabled. Some USB ports support power switching independent of port enable.

Item.Name: Current Draw
Item.Description: Current drawn by the connected USB device in milliamperes. USB 2.0 standard allows up to 500mA, USB 3.0 up to 900mA, USB-C can provide higher currents.
